_comment: Water & sanitation protocol for incident_type=water_sanitation. Operational guidance for trained responders, paraphrased from the Sphere Handbook WASH minimum standards (indicator values are the public Sphere/WHO figures, stated in our own words). No patient diagnosis or treatment. Loaded by matcher.py at startup; keywords drive the forgiving fallback match on needs/notes.
title: Water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH)

guidance.action: Plan for at least about 15 litres of safe water per person per day for drinking, cooking, and hygiene, and keep water points within roughly 500 m of shelters.
guidance.rationale: This is the Sphere basic survival quantity; below it, dehydration and hygiene-related disease climb sharply.

guidance.action: Make water potable before distribution: bring it to a rolling boil for about one minute, or chlorinate to a free chlorine residual of about 0.2-0.5 mg/L at the point of use; let cloudy water settle or filter it first.
guidance.rationale: Untreated water in a contaminated zone is the main driver of diarrhoeal disease, and chlorine works poorly in turbid water.

guidance.action: Site latrines at least 30 m from any water source, with the pit bottom about 1.5 m above the water table, and downhill of wells.
guidance.rationale: Physical separation is what prevents faecal contamination of the drinking-water supply.

guidance.action: Provide at least one latrine per 20 people, separated by sex, lockable from inside, and lit at night.
guidance.rationale: Adequate, safe sanitation coverage curbs open defecation and keeps latrines usable by women and children.

guidance.action: Set up handwashing points with soap (or ash if soap is unavailable) at every latrine, water point, and food area.
guidance.rationale: Handwashing is the single highest-leverage barrier to faecal-oral transmission.

guidance.action: Protect stored water: distribute in covered, narrow-necked containers, keep collection vessels clean, and pour rather than dip.
guidance.rationale: Safely treated water is commonly re-contaminated in the home; storage discipline preserves the treatment effort.

safety_flags: Verify free chlorine residual before distributing treated water
Keep latrines at least 30 m from, and downhill of, water sources
Treat all post-earthquake piped water as suspect until tested
